Royco is quietly becoming core infrastructure for STRC yield onchain, a credit vault that dynamically prices risk between tranches, and now Pendle sits on top of that stack as a yield trading/fixed yield layer.

P.S. srRoyAPYUSD on Pendle soon 😉

jrRoyAPYUSD: https://t.co/yv24hxVnyJ

TL;DR

jrRoyAPYUSD → Amplified yield, first-loss seat. You earn the premium + absorb the drawdown (if any) https://t.co/GSKBV63H3A

When apyUSD drawdowns, the pool doesn't immediately crystallize a loss.

It opens a 30-day Observation Period instead. Senior withdrawals pause. Yield flows entirely to Junior.

Recover within the window → Nothing happens

Don't recover → jrRoyAPYUSD takes the hit

The yields aren't static and are priced dynamically based on Utilization.

Utilization tracks how stretched Junior's protective capacity is. Think of it like a borrow rate curve.

Past 90%, the risk premium Senior pays Junior steepens sharply to attract more Junior capital.

Any

jrRoyAPYUSD captures a premium paid by Seniors for drawdown protection, earning the underlying rate plus a premium for the risk they take on.

In return, you'll also be the first line of defense when apyUSD takes a hit.
Current APY: 30.42% (7D avg: 19.31%) https://t.co/CHd6cwo6WM

Structured yield meets fixed yield for @saylor's STRC.

RT @SherifDefi: The CLARITY Act has nearly 70% chance of passing according to Polymarket.

CT is still talking about what it means for $BTC…

This is what the maturing RWA landscape looks like. Structured tranches, defined risk tiers, predictable yield...

Pendle is committed to being the fixed yield layer for RWAs as the asset class continues to grow, enabling yields to be speculated and traded separately, as in the https://t.co/n80Xzxx8TQ

TL;DR

srUSDat → Protected until Junior is fully drained. Yield is lower, but your principal is insulated from STRC price swings.

jrUSDat → You're the buffer. You earn more, but you absorb STRC depegs before anyone else does. https://t.co/fapsA1GFe8

One key number to watch 👇🏻

Coverage Ratio = Total vault / Senior vault.

The minimum safe threshold is 107.5%. Below that, srUSDat minting and jrUSDat redemption are temporarily halted until the threshold recovers.

Below 100% is when jrUSDat is wiped out and when srUSDat starts

For instance, a mere 1% drop in STRC's price can translate to a 3-4% drop in jrUSDat's exchange rate, depending on the ratio between the two tranches.

When this happens, jrUSDat will be below the Watermark Rate, and yield accrual for YT will be paused until the Watermark https://t.co/StC2jUNi1f

Since the Senior tranche is typically much larger, that leftover pile divided across a small Junior base produces amplified yield - typically ~25% APR.

Do note however that jrUSDat will also be the first to absorb losses when STRC's price drops.

srUSDat earns 65% of STRC's dividend yield. Yield floats with whatever STRC pays

e.g. 11.5% APR x 65% = ~7.5% APR

In other words, your yield is lower, but your principal is insulated from STRC price swings.

jrUSDat earns everything left over (i.e. 35%) after the Senior is

Both pools are backed by sUSDat, @saturn_credit's yield bearing asset that holds STRC as collateral.

STRC pays a dividend. That dividend is split between two tranches: Senior (srUSDat) and Junior (jrUSDat).

Senior gets paid first, and Junior gets the rest. https://t.co/sM80Sgshd3

.@Saylor's STRC has been quietly building one of the most compelling RWA yield ecosystems onchain

- jrUSDat (Aug 2026) https://t.co/IFK2UgIWj9
